title: Fix Windows career command config-guard compilation
description: The production Windows Tauri build fails in seven career query commands because active_identity receives &lock(...)?, causing the ? operator to be type-checked against AppConfig rather than the MutexGuard deref. Correct the guard handling without changing career behavior and verify the Windows production build.
acceptance_criteria: Windows Tauri production build compiles; career command identity lookup retains the same validation; Rust/frontend quality gates pass; installed Windows app restarts from the production executable.
close_reason: Corrected active-character identity lookup through a scoped config guard; 320 Rust tests and the Windows Tauri production build pass, and the installed app was smoke-tested at tauri.localhost.

title: Repair v0.2.15 release lockfile version
description: The v0.2.15 version bump updated async-signal in Cargo.lock instead of the eqlogs-app package entry because both were 0.2.14. Restore async-signal 0.2.14, set the app package consistently to the next release, and publish a valid Windows installer.
acceptance_criteria: Cargo.lock preserves registry dependency versions/checksums; eqlogs-app version matches package/Tauri metadata; Windows release workflow succeeds and installed app reports the repaired version.
notes: Restored async-signal lock entry to 0.2.14 and set the eqlogs-app lock entry plus all release metadata to 0.2.16. npm build/tests pass (133), and Windows cargo check --locked passes.
close_reason: v0.2.16 release succeeded and was installed on Windows

title: Release Legends Companion v0.2.6
description: Publish the completed trigger editor, modular overlay, severity policy, and Impact overlay improvements through the signed Windows GitHub release pipeline and updater channel.
acceptance_criteria: All app version manifests and lockfiles are 0.2.6; frontend, pack, and Rust release gates pass; release commit and v0.2.6 tag are pushed; GitHub release workflow succeeds; installer and latest.json are published; repository is clean and synced.
close_reason: Released Legends Companion v0.2.6; Windows installer and updater manifest published, CI and release workflows passed.

title: Cross-validate every quest item source
description: Extend the quest acquisition-source pipeline beyond Plane of Sky so every required and reward item carries claim-level provenance from at least two independent sources where available. Record agreement, conflicts, single-source gaps, and source freshness without presenting uncorroborated data as verified.
acceptance_criteria: Catalog audits every unique quest item; each acquisition claim exposes independent source count and agreement status; UI distinguishes corroborated, single-source, conflicting, and unresolved claims; refresh tooling and tests cover source provenance and licensing.

title: Expand Plane of Sky quest item source coverage
description: Many required Plane of Sky quest items exist in the quest catalog but have no matching item/drop origin in the bundled classic reference database. Add a provenance-aware source layer and additional reputable data sources so quest requirements can show mob, island/zone, or quest/crafted origin with scalable refresh tooling.
acceptance_criteria: Missing Sky quest components display verified source information where available; source provenance is retained; normalization/alias handling is tested; unresolved items are distinguishable from confirmed non-mob-drop items.

title: Add icons and cleanup behavior to compact combat alerts
description: Compact Alerts overlay messages such as melee skill damage and spell resists lack visual identity. Add maintained icons to skill triggers, support data-driven spell-name icon resolution for captured spell alerts, and configure outgoing resist triggers to clear both pending and target-bound spell timers.
acceptance_criteria: Kick and other maintained melee alerts render configured icons; outgoing resist alerts resolve the captured spell's gem icon; resisted spells are removed from the target overlay through trigger actions; frontend and trigger-pack tests pass.

title: Preserve spell icons when timers bind to targets
description: Target binding renames active DoT, debuff, and buff-on-other timer rows and re-emits StartTimer outside trigger scope, dropping the trigger icon and rendering the stopwatch fallback. Carry existing timer icon metadata through generic rebind/start emissions and add regression coverage for each binding path.
acceptance_criteria: DoT damage binding, non-damaging debuff landing binding, and buff-on-other binding retain the original spell icon in emitted timer rows; timer lifecycle tests pass; Windows build is deployed and verified.

title: Refresh active timer icons after trigger updates
description: Hot-rebuilding the trigger engine drops backend timers while existing frontend rows remain, so rows created before an icon metadata update keep fallback clocks until recast. Emit the rebuilt trigger-driven timer icon map and update matching live rows without changing countdowns.
acceptance_criteria: Installing a trigger update immediately replaces fallback clocks on matching active timer rows using the new trigger metadata; countdown timing is unchanged; no spell names or icon IDs are hardcoded.
close_reason: Active countdown rows now adopt the hot-rebuilt engine's literal trigger icon map without changing timing; validated live with Togor's Insects and Malosi.

title: Validate and align spell gem icons
description: Spell gem icons shown in timer overlays may be mapped to the wrong client icon or cropped off-grid, and Cascading Darkness currently falls back to a clock. Validate installed spell metadata, every referenced PNG crop, and timer payload propagation; fix mapping/cropping and missing metadata.
acceptance_criteria: Timer overlay shows the correct aligned spell gem for Cascading Darkness and representative ranked spells; referenced spell icon PNGs are 25x25, nonblank, grid-aligned, and mapped from the correct spells_us field; automated tests cover mapping and crop behavior.
close_reason: Corrected NEW_ICON parsing to caret field 75, 24px gemicons crops, regenerated all packs, corrected curated mappings, and validated all 119 referenced PNGs plus Windows tests.

title: Preserve spell icons with downloaded trigger libraries
description: Downloaded trigger packs can predate bundled trigger metadata and shadow icon fields, causing live timer overlays to render no spell gems even though the app and client artwork support them. Hydrate missing portable icon metadata from same-id bundled triggers and publish a fresh rolling trigger library.
acceptance_criteria: Same-id downloaded triggers retain their behavior but inherit a missing bundled icon; explicit downloaded icons are preserved; live timer payloads render spell gems; rolling trigger data includes current icon metadata.
close_reason: Published trigger library 2026-07-14.1 with spell icons; added and tested bundled-icon hydration for older downloaded libraries.

title: Add ranked timer retraining diagnostics
description: Add a Diagnostics workflow that analyzes historical casts for ranked abilities, rejects inconsistent samples, estimates a new duration, supports a test run, and only applies an explicit timer override after review.
acceptance_criteria: Diagnostics can select a rank-aware timer, inspect consistent historical samples, preview an estimated duration without mutation, and explicitly apply the override; tests cover rank parsing, consistency, estimation, and no-op test runs.
close_reason: Added a read-only ranked timer training scan in Diagnostics with consistency/outlier checks, explicit per-rank Apply to the active loadout, and five backend tests; Windows tests and frontend build pass.

title: Fix 3.0.1 CI clippy failure
description: Release CI rejects execute_actions after ObserveWatch added an eighth argument. Remove the redundant flag by deriving watch-observation eligibility from raw capture versus structured field template values, then rerun clippy and CI.
acceptance_criteria: cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes; Windows app clippy passes; fix is pushed and CI succeeds.
notes: Removed redundant ObserveWatch eligibility argument by deriving raw-vs-structured eligibility from TemplateValues; also updated pre-existing career progress Option check for current Clippy. cargo fmt, workspace Clippy -D warnings, eqlog-triggers tests, and Windows-native app Clippy -D warnings all pass.
close_reason: Both CI Clippy failures fixed and exact local/Windows gates pass.

title: Make loot and kill watch observations trigger-configurable
description: Move raw loot and slain recognition into curated event-source triggers with a configurable Observe action that advances watch goals and emits structured watched signals.
acceptance_criteria: Raw loot/kill formats are editable trigger JSON; no typed parser watch advancement remains; editor supports Observe action; tests prove alternate raw patterns work without source changes.
close_reason: Implemented configurable ObserveWatch action, trigger-authored event source pack, safe structured signal queue, editor UI, and native/engine/frontend coverage.

title: Publish Legends Companion 3.0 release
description: Add the approved 3.0 patch notes covering career history and recent major features, update application version metadata, run release quality gates, publish the Windows release, and deploy/restart it on the Windows machine.
acceptance_criteria: PATCH_NOTES.md begins with the approved 3.0 entry; app version metadata is 3.0.0; checks pass; release is published; Windows app is restarted on the live build; changes and beads are pushed.
close_reason: Published v3.0.0 patch notes and version metadata, passed frontend/workspace/Windows checks, published signed GitHub installer and updater manifest, and deployed a verified production-asset Windows build.

title: Trigger-driven item watches and quest loot alerts
description: Evolve the existing localStorage wishlist into character-scoped, quantity-aware item watches. Users can add/remove watches from Drops and individual quest requirements, bulk-watch or unwatch all missing requirements for a quest, and optionally remove a watch when its required quantity is looted. A typed watched-loot trigger must route configurable actions to the modular Impact overlay, which presents a chest-opening treatment and 'You looted {item}', without hard-coded item parsing or direct TTS/toast calls in session tracking.
design: Migrate eqlogs.wishlist.v1 entries into a character-scoped WatchStore keyed by canonical item identity. Model one item with multiple goals (manual or quest id), required/owned/remaining quantities, enabled state, and auto-remove-on-goal-complete. Match the parser's typed Loot event exactly against WatchStore; emit a structured watched-loot trigger context (item, quantity, corpse, remaining, quest names) through the normal trigger action executor. Ship one editable default trigger with an Impact overlay action using a loot-chest presentation plus PlaySound; keep TTS optional. Reconcile /output inventory without firing alerts. Drops retains a one-click watch control and gains a Watched filter/manager; Quests gains per-requirement controls plus Watch missing items and Remove quest watches. Reuse a single item entry across multiple quest goals and aggregate quantities.
acceptance_criteria: Drops and Quests can add/remove item watches; quest bulk action accounts for inventory-owned quantities and repeated requirements; watched Loot events fire once through trigger actions with exact item/quantity template data; Impact shows the animated chest and 'You looted {item}'; auto-remove occurs only when the configured quantity goal is met; inventory refresh reconciles silently; similar item names do not false-match; multiple linked quests do not duplicate alerts; legacy wishlist entries migrate; watches persist per character; focused Rust/frontend tests, production build, and Windows smoke pass.
notes: Implemented character-scoped JSON WatchStore, exact typed self-loot matching, structured watched-loot trigger actions, modular loot-chest Impact presentation, Drops manager, per-item and bulk Quest controls, inventory reconciliation, legacy migration, quantity/auto-remove controls, and old trigger-library compatibility. Verified frontend build + 253 tests, trigger crate 156 tests, Windows backend 24 tests, Windows Clippy (with one unrelated career lint exemption), production Windows build, install, and tauri.localhost smoke.
close_reason: Closed

title: Windows-verify: career DB (career.rs module, async commands, migration, import flow)
description: Career-db src-tauri edits need Windows build + smoke: (1) src-tauri compiles — esp. #[tauri::command(async)] on the 7 career query/reset commands (only compile-unverified edit); (2) launch logs 'career history opened', existing v1 fights.db migrates with Fights tab intact; (3) Settings > General > Career history: Import shows per-file progress bars, completion toast, lastImportAt; re-run says already up to date; (4) Session tab Career + Loot ledger panels render sane dates (camelCase seam), search/pager/mob-expand/deep-links work; (5) UI responsive when opening Session tab mid-full-import (async fix); (6) concurrent import returns 'already running'; (7) tail-start import silent, logged only on new bytes; (8) reset -> pills 0 -> re-import rebuilds; (9) quit removes fights.db -wal/-shm sidecars; (10) careerGapMins=5 in settings.json splits more sessions. Follow-ups: visible Settings control for careerGapMins; distinguish benign skip reasons from real per-file failures in the import status line.

title: Track enemy damage sources in fight details
description: Fight detail currently renders incoming damage grouped by victim. Change persistence and UI so the enemy panel shows each enemy as a combatant row with expandable melee skills, spells, and effects, matching the outgoing player damage structure. Preserve compatibility with stored fights that lack enemy source rows.
close_reason: Enemy damage is now stored by attacking mob and source, rendered expanded in fight detail, and covered by parser/store/UI verification.

title: Improve quest inventory filters, item coverage, trigger icons, and direct search navigation
description: Implement four linked UX/data improvements: optionally hide quests whose documented final reward is already owned (not quests whose required materials are owned), ensure quest-required items such as Large Sky Sapphire appear in Drops research even without known drop rows, allow every trigger to carry an icon and populate bundled trigger icons from spell-gem data, and make global search selections open the destination detail directly.
design: Reuse the existing reference database, deep-link events, trigger schema/action presentation, and bundled spell icon assets. Preserve unknown drop-source truth rather than inventing locations.
acceptance_criteria: Quests has a clear inventory-based omit-owned option; every quest requirement is discoverable in Drops with honest unknown-source handling; trigger editor/actions can select an icon and bundled spell/buff/timer alerts use the appropriate gem icon; global search item/mob/quest/etc selections open the selected detail without an intermediate search action; focused tests and production build pass.
close_reason: Implemented final-reward inventory filtering, unsourced quest-item visibility, portable spell-gem trigger icons with generated/curated defaults, and exact global-search detail navigation; all quality gates pass.

title: Windows-verify: library.rs zones field + commands.rs share update-in-place
description: Two src-tauri edits from the feature round need Windows build + smoke (src-tauri does not compile in WSL): (1) library.rs TriggerTreeEntry gained pub zones: Vec<String> + tree_entry() clone — verify zone badges render and loadout zone_scopes save; (2) commands.rs share_export/share_export_file use ..Default::default() for new SharePayload metadata, share_read_file forwards version/author/notes, share_import gained update_in_place: Option<bool> + updated count — verify pasting the same share string twice shows the diff dialog, update-in-place creates no -2 copies, and per-trigger overrides survive. Also visual pass: welcome-back card, session analytics panels, zone chips, scoreboard Mez column.

title: Session IA merge: one Session concept, Fights=history only, one timers surface
description: 'Session' means 3 things (Coach tab, topbar tailing popover, FightsTab session card). Move FightsTab SessionSection accumulation into lib stores, absorb its 8 sub-tabs into Session (Coach) tab, delete CoachTab duplicates (PaceRates/kills/effects rendered twice), reduce FightsTab to fight history, remove MetersTab Active-timers card, rename topbar popover to Tailing, drop redundant Collapsible.
close_reason: Closed

title: Extract OverlayShell + useOverlayLock; single overlay arrange state
description: All 11 overlays copy-paste ~30-line lock scaffold with drift: only 5 listen to OVERLAY_ARRANGE_KEY fallback; Dashboard/SettingsTab hold unsynced arrange state (3 controls that lie). Extract OverlayShell wrapper + useOverlayLock hook + shared useOverlayArrange store (pattern of refFilters). Also: derive OVERLAY_LABELS from OVERLAY_MODULES, unify body-drag via imperative startDragging, fluid pill widths.
close_reason: Closed

title: Quick-fix batch: first-run flow, CSS token repairs, destructive-action confirms, Meters deaths tile
description: From full UX review. (1) First run: auto-start tailing after WelcomeCard log pick + Start CTA in Live empty state + WelcomeCard rescan/poll for logs. (2) styles.css: define/replace --ok/--danger with --good/--critical, fix .tmr-zone-pick>.search-select→.sselect, define --accent-ink, dead CSS sweep (~300 lines), series-palette leaks in chrome, pill token extraction. (3) Undo toast for per-fight delete, confirm for loadout delete, replace window.prompt tell-parse. (4) Meters Deaths tile scoped per-fight/relabeled.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix item resource IDs and link Drops items to quests
description: ProjectEQ item IDs are being sent to Allakhazam as live item IDs, which can open an unrelated item. Use name-based item lookup for external resources and show exact quest requirement cross-references in expanded Drops item details.
acceptance_criteria: Silvery Ring ZAM link searches by name rather than opening item 20700; expanded Silvery Ring shows Test of Smash as a quest use; quest link opens the filtered Quests tab; spell ID behavior remains unchanged; tests cover URL generation and exact quest matching.
close_reason: Fixed ProjectEQ/Allakhazam item ID mismatch and added exact Drops-to-Quests cross-references with navigation and tests.

title: Filter quests ready for completion from inventory
description: Add a quest-page filter that shows only quests whose documented required item quantities are all satisfied by the loaded /output inventory snapshot.
acceptance_criteria: Quest toolbar offers an obvious Ready filter; active filter keeps only quests with at least one documented requirement and all quantities satisfied; filter is disabled or explained without inventory; counts update immediately after inventory refresh; tests cover ready, incomplete, quantity, and no-requirement cases.
notes: Added inventory-aware Ready filter beside zone/class with contextual count. It requires at least one documented requirement and all quantities satisfied, updates after inventory refresh, disables without inventory, and filters after search/zone/class but before the 150-row display cap. Verified desktop/mobile layout, 134 tests, production build, and Windows cargo check --locked.
close_reason: Implemented and verified for v0.2.17

title: Make global item search reveal unsourced Drops results
description: Global search can return item reference rows with zero known drop sources, but opening them in Drops preserves the only-sourced filter and shows no matches. Ensure explicit item navigation reveals the selected reference while keeping normal persisted filter behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Opening an unsourced item from global search displays it in Drops; normal Drops searches retain user filter preferences; sourced results continue to work; tests cover explicit unsourced navigation.
notes: Confirmed Silvery Ring (item 20700) is a valid item reference with zero drop NPCs while Elegant Silvery Ring is sourced. Global item actions now carry revealUnsourced for zero-source selections; Drops disables onlySourced only for that explicit navigation. Added tests for sourced and unsourced actions.
close_reason: Fixed and verified for v0.2.15

title: Add drop sources and reward tooltips to quest items
description: Enhance quest cards so every required item shows known drop sources from the bundled reference database, and reward items expose the existing item detail/drop information on hover.
acceptance_criteria: Required quest items show known NPC/zone drop sources or a clear unknown state; reward items have keyboard-accessible hover/focus details; interaction reuses existing drop/item reference APIs and communicates classic-reference caveats; loading remains bounded and tests cover lookup/missing items.
notes: Implemented one bounded batch lookup for visible quest requirement/reward names using the bundled drop database. Required items show up to two NPC/zone/chance sources plus remaining count or explicit unknown guidance. Reward names expose keyboard-accessible hover/focus tooltips with stats, effects, flags, level, and drop summary. Added formatting tests and verified 529-name default query in ~11 ms.
close_reason: Implemented and verified for v0.2.14

title: Import and reconcile /outputfile inventory with quest requirements
description: Discover and parse EverQuest inventory output exports, aggregate owned item quantities across locations, and match them to structured quest requirements so quest UI can show owned/required and completed items. Preserve last successful snapshot, surface file age/path, and handle aliases/duplicate names without silently guessing.
acceptance_criteria: User can select or auto-discover an inventory output file; parser handles representative export rows and quantities; normalized inventory totals cross-reference quest item requirements; quest UI checks completed requirements and shows owned/required; stale/missing/malformed exports fail visibly and retain the previous good snapshot; tests cover bags, bank/shared bank, stacks, duplicates, aliases, and unknown items.
notes: Implemented auto-discovery/manual selection of /output inventory, section-aware TSV parsing including appended KeyRing table, ID-first/exact normalized-name matching, quantities/locations, persisted last-good snapshots, stale/missing/malformed feedback, and required-item checkmarks. Validated against Nyasha_oggok-Inventory.txt.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in v0.2.13

title: Consolidate Session overview into three-column comparison workspace
description: Replace the scattered Session Overview cards with one aligned Current Fight, Current Session, and Baseline Session comparison workspace. Show progress/combat metrics and top skills left-to-right, keep baseline selection in the header, reduce manual pace controls, and move history/routes/setup details out of Overview.
acceptance_criteria: Desktop Overview has three aligned scope columns; XP, AA, motes, damage/DPS, kills, deaths, and downtime are scannable left-to-right; top skills compare in the same columns; empty states remain compact; previous sessions, route details, mobs, and setup diagnostics do not clutter Overview; responsive layout remains usable; tests/build pass.
close_reason: Replaced scattered Overview cards with aligned Current Fight, Current Session, and Baseline columns; added live session skill/rate folding, compact AA measurement, and moved route detail out of Overview.

title: Add complete quest database with hail-time quest giver lookup
description: Build a maintainable quest dataset covering all known quests and associate quest givers with their quest details so hailing an NPC can surface relevant quest information in the app. Plane of Sky quests are mandatory and must receive explicit completeness verification. Keep data provenance/update workflows clear so the quest database can be refreshed as game knowledge changes.
acceptance_criteria: Quest records and quest-giver associations are queryable in-app; hailing a known quest giver surfaces relevant quest summaries and requirements; Plane of Sky quest coverage is explicitly audited and complete; unknown NPCs fail gracefully; dataset provenance and refresh workflow are documented; tests cover lookup, multiple quests per NPC, aliases, and missing data.
notes: Implemented 985-record EQL Wiki catalog with page/revision provenance, 94 audited Plane of Sky quests across all 16 classes, exact giver/alias hail lookup, quest browse/search UI, and global search integration. Generator documented under tools/questdata.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in v0.2.13

title: Build session comparison domain
description: Create a pure TypeScript comparison model for Coach session snapshots, including goal-aware metrics, baseline deltas, sample confidence, and concise driver findings.
acceptance_criteria: Focused tests cover XP, AA, motes, damage, missing/zero baselines, confidence labels, and driver findings. No CoachTab or Dashboard edits.
notes: Implemented app/src/lib/sessionComparison.ts plus 14 focused tests. Full npm test (122 tests) and npm run build pass; existing Vite chunk warnings remain.
close_reason: Pure comparison domain and focused coverage complete; ready for CoachTab integration by parent task.

title: Add session efficiency comparisons
description: Unify current fight/current session/past session viewing with a selectable previous or pinned baseline, goal-aware progress/combat/route comparisons, confidence, and concise explanatory findings.
close_reason: Implemented current fight/current session/past session comparisons with automatic or pinned baselines, XP/AA/mote/damage goals, confidence, route and skill efficiency, session-wide damage sources including damage shields, responsive UI, and tests.

title: Make Rates discoverable in Session
description: Rename the Insights navigation destination to Session and expose the XP/AA/mote pace workflow as a first-class Session view synchronized with live Fights ingestion.
close_reason: Renamed Insights to Session, made Rates the default Session view, extracted a shared PaceRates component, synchronized pace state with background Fights ingestion, retained Fights access, and verified the wide layout visually.

title: Remove hardcoded proc classification
description: Eliminate heuristic proc labeling from Fights/session effects so spells such as Tremor remain spells and trigger-driven alerts stay independent; preserve skill classification and session export compatibility.
close_reason: Removed item-database proc classification, built-in proc/skill/spell Alerts and TTS, proc preference storage, and Alerts-overlay listener. Session/Insights now consume structured SpellDamage analytics only; Tremor regression covered; automation remains trigger-owned.

title: Publish pace and timing release
description: Bump and publish the next Legends Companion release containing common pace tracking, ranked spell timing, and contextual respawn profiles; verify updater assets and Windows runtime.
close_reason: Published signed v0.2.8 Windows installer and updater manifest, refreshed data-latest to 2026-07-12.1, compiled and restarted Windows debug app, and verified release assets.

title: Unify rolling XP rate with pace domain
description: Move the existing rolling ten-minute active-time XP calculation behind the common pace domain while preserving the overlayState API and behavior.
close_reason: Moved rolling 10-minute active-time XP rate into generic pace-domain calculator; retained overlayState compatibility adapter and existing behavior. All 106 frontend tests and production build pass.

title: Add contextual respawn timing profiles
description: Add public/private/custom respawn contexts, persistent per-mob and per-zone timing overrides, manual pop calibration, source badges, and unified overlay/list behavior.
close_reason: Added persistent public/private/custom respawn timing profiles, reference/manual/observed resolution, per-context calibration UI, timer provenance, overlay context badges, migration-safe fields, and focused tests.

title: Resolve ranked spell timer timing
description: Capture spell ranks, add reusable ranked duration/cast-time timing profiles and manual overrides, resolve them in the trigger engine, update generated packs/editor UI, and add regression tests.
close_reason: Added direct active-loadout timing editor for bundled/generated timer rows, discoverable badges, per-rank clear/reset, persistence helper, and tests.

title: Add pace controls and modular overlay
description: Add Session Rates controls, manual AA sample workflow, automatic XP/AA/mote display, history, session export integration, and a compact configurable Pace overlay.
close_reason: Implemented persisted Rates workflow, live XP/AA/Mote ingestion, sample history/export payload, and registered modular Pace overlay with responsive UI. Frontend tests/build and visual checks pass.

title: Build pace domain and AA parsing
description: Add a structured AA-point event, common rate/sample state and calculations for XP, AA, and configurable loot metrics, persistence, and focused tests.
close_reason: Added structured AA-point parsing plus tested common pace state, rates, configurable loot metrics, replay suppression, history, and persistence contracts.

title: Unified pace engine and timing profiles
description: Implement shared rate tracking for XP, AA, motes, and future metrics plus shared timing resolution for ranked spells, manual calibration, and context-specific respawns.
close_reason: Delivered unified rolling and sampled pace tracking, structured AA and configurable loot rates, modular Pace overlay, ranked spell timing with active-loadout overrides, and contextual respawn calibration with modular overlay output.

title: Make invis dropping a loud large default alert
description: Update the default universal invis-dropping trigger to produce an alarm-tier large Alerts overlay message and danger sound while preserving shared invis timer cancellation; add regression coverage and publish the trigger data update.
close_reason: Diagnosed Windows runtime loading stale 2026-07-11 refdata override. Atomically installed verified 2026-07-11.1 archive, confirmed no Nyasha loadout overrides, verified loud default action on disk, and restarted app at 2026-07-12 01:02 local.

title: Release Legends Companion v0.2.7
description: Version, commit, push, tag, and verify a live Windows release containing independent trigger updates, full trigger package sharing, and session export.
close_reason: Released v0.2.7 publicly with signed Windows installer and updater manifest; main/tag CI and release workflow passed.

title: Export complete play sessions
description: Add a file export for a complete recorded play session using existing fight/session data, with a stable shareable format and clear UI entry point.
close_reason: Implemented versioned current-session JSON export with native save dialog, privacy-safe structured session collections, and complete overlapping stored fight summaries; verified frontend, store, Clippy, formatting, and Windows compilation.

title: Import and export complete trigger setups
description: Add a portable Companion trigger package that exports all trigger definitions plus applicable loadout configuration and can be safely imported on another installation without overwriting local custom data.
notes: Frontend complete: Triggers tab Export all .lct action, Companion package import picker feeding existing safe preview/import dialog, native package filename presentation, and shareExportFile/shareReadFile API wrappers. npm build and 85 tests pass; backend integration remains in shared worktree.
close_reason: Added lossless full-library .lct export and safe package import with local-id filtering and preview.

title: Add trigger library update UX
description: Expose a clear trigger-library updater in the Triggers tab so users can check for and install the latest shared triggers independently of app releases.
acceptance_criteria: Triggers tab shows installed and available trigger library versions/status, supports checking and installing updates through the frontend API, handles loading/error/current states, and remains usable at narrow layouts.
close_reason: Dedicated trigger-library updater added to Triggers tab with version, check/install states, event refresh, responsive styling, and passing frontend gates.

title: Add independent trigger library updater backend
description: Add a dedicated Tauri backend update path for trigger libraries with independent version tracking, verified triggers.zip download, atomic installation, and immediate live-engine refresh.
acceptance_criteria: Backend can check and install the latest trigger library independently of drops/reference data; install is verified and atomic; active engine reloads after install; tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented independent verified trigger-library updates with version tracking, atomic install, live engine reload, and lossless .lct backend commands; Windows check and focused tests pass.

title: Publish refreshed 1,750-trigger data pack
description: Rebuild dist-data triggers.zip from the v0.2.6 trigger library, update the data manifest version and checksums, publish the data-latest assets, and verify upgraded installs receive all 1,750 triggers.
close_reason: Published and verified data-latest version 2026-07-11 with 1,750 triggers.

title: Fix clipped Impact slash number
description: The finishing-blow slash animation leaves its focal number rendered as two clipped halves, creating a persistent seam that reads as clipping. Restore the intact number after the cleave animation while preserving the transient split effect.
acceptance_criteria: Slash style visibly splits during animation and settles to a seamless intact number; large 3- and 4-digit values do not clip; reduced-motion remains clean; frontend build/tests and visual check pass; changes committed and pushed.
close_reason: Adjusted the slash animation to use clipped halves only during the cleave, then restore the intact number; added strike padding and verified mid/final 1,204 frames without clipping.

title: Scale Impact overlay to a half-screen focal moment
description: The Impact overlay is currently a 380x210 window with 62-74px focal type. Increase its default transparent window and responsive typography so finishing blows, level-ups, crits, badges, and medals read as large half-screen moments while remaining configurable and nonblocking.
acceptance_criteria: Impact defaults to an approximately half-screen focal footprint at 1080p; every supported style scales responsively without clipping; arrange/lock behavior remains usable; desktop visual checks cover slash, big-number, level, badge, and medal; frontend/Tauri gates pass; changes committed and pushed.
close_reason: Scaled Impact to a 960x540 half-screen default with a one-time migration from the legacy 380x210 persisted size, responsive container breakpoints for all five styles, and verified slash/big-number/level/badge/medal screenshots.

title: Normalize trigger alert severity defaults
description: Apply a consistent bundled-trigger severity policy: routine spell and damage alerts are quiet, crowd-control alerts are warn, and dangerous failures that can lead to death are loud. Cover packs, generators, templates, starter defaults, and tests without rewriting users' customized triggers.
acceptance_criteria: All shipped/default trigger sources use the policy consistently; routine spell/damage defaults resolve to info/quiet; crowd-control defaults resolve to warn; dangerous failures including failed feign resolve to alarm/loud; user trigger files are not migrated or overwritten; tests cover representative cases; build/test gates pass; changes committed and pushed.
close_reason: Normalized alert defaults: routine spells/damage and generated non-CC wear-offs are info, all recognized/generated CC is warn, failed/broken/ended Feign and survival failures are alarm; added policy tests and pack validation without mutating user triggers.

title: Polish trigger editor form controls
description: Refine the trigger editor's individual controls so selects, textareas, inputs, labels, focus states, and compact field layouts feel cohesive and intentional in the Windows desktop app.
acceptance_criteria: Trigger editor selects and textareas no longer look like raw browser controls; field labels, focus states, spacing, and dropdown presentation are consistent; keyboard behavior and existing serialization remain unchanged; desktop and narrow visual checks pass; frontend build and tests pass; changes are committed and pushed.
close_reason: Polished trigger editor segmented choices, selects, inputs, textareas, labels, focus/hover states, responsive field stacking, and opaque sticky controls; desktop and narrow visual checks plus frontend gates pass.

title: Redesign trigger editor for action-first usability
description: Turn the trigger editor into a focused workspace with clear When/Then flow, collapsible typed action cards, an explicit Add Action menu, progressive overlay configuration, live summaries/previews, and a sticky save footer. Hide unsupported webhook authoring while preserving existing webhook data.
acceptance_criteria: Editing replaces the library workspace instead of nesting among it; actions have typed headers/summaries and collapse/expand; Add Action presents explicit choices and never defaults silently; overlay content is primary and appearance is progressive; legacy/unknown actions remain lossless; starter creates generic Overlay actions; webhook cannot be newly authored while desktop delivery is unavailable; desktop/narrow screenshots have no overlap; Rust/frontend gates pass; changes committed and pushed.
close_reason: Redesigned the trigger editor around focused action cards, explicit action selection, progressive overlay settings, responsive layout, and lossless legacy handling; build and tests pass.

title: Give user triggers stable identities
description: User triggers without explicit ids derive identity from category and name. Renaming or moving a trigger changes its id and silently orphans enable/channel/severity/zone overrides.
acceptance_criteria: New user triggers receive a stable id on first save; edits preserve it; existing derived-id triggers migrate without losing overrides; tests cover rename and category move.

title: Make duplicate-trigger suppression explicit
description: The engine currently deduplicates independent triggers solely because their expanded regex patterns are identical. This prevents two separately managed triggers from routing the same match to different outputs.
acceptance_criteria: Independent triggers with identical patterns can both fire; optional explicit dedupe group/policy suppresses intentional duplicates; import spam protection remains available; tests cover identical pattern fan-out.

title: Align trigger and loadout schemas across Rust and TypeScript
description: Frontend types and editor/profile operations omit Rust fields including trigger zones, timer cast_time_secs, loadout zone_scopes, and advanced timer behavior. Loadout duplication also drops channel/severity/scoping behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Rust/TS contract fixtures cover all serialized fields; editing without changes is lossless; duplicating a loadout deep-copies all behavior; schema drift fails CI.
close_reason: Implemented explicit action ownership, complete overlay/timer/profile schema alignment, lossless editor round-trips, and loadout cloning tests.

title: Make trigger action attribution explicit
description: The Tauri sink currently reconstructs action ownership from per-trigger action counts after processing. No-op/host-unsupported actions, overrides, and engine housekeeping can desynchronize the buffer and misattribute later actions. Carry trigger identity with each emitted action directly.
design: Prefer an engine output envelope or begin/end trigger context over action_counts arithmetic.
acceptance_criteria: Every user action event carries the correct trigger id/name without count partitioning; housekeeping remains explicitly unattributed; tests cover multiple triggers, channel overrides, unsupported host actions, and mixed action types.
close_reason: Implemented explicit action ownership, complete overlay/timer/profile schema alignment, lossless editor round-trips, and loadout cloning tests.

title: Make trigger editor lossless and destination-first
description: Rework action authoring around simple destinations: TTS, overlays, sounds, timers, and integrations. Preserve every supported action field when editing/importing; make multiple destinations obvious; show destination-specific presentation controls and a faithful live preview.
design: Keep the current intent-first match builder. Replace the single action-kind mental model with an Add destination flow and advanced per-destination settings.
acceptance_criteria: Opening and saving a trigger without edits is schema-lossless; users can select multiple overlay destinations without duplicating the match; preview shows expanded payload per destination; unsupported fields are preserved or explicitly surfaced; tests cover all action variants.
close_reason: Implemented destination-first registry-driven overlay editing, legacy migration, lossless advanced timer preservation, destination chips, previews, and codec tests.

title: Implement or remove webhook trigger actions
description: PostWebhook is exposed in TriggerEditor but the desktop EmitSink inherits a no-op implementation and Settings has no webhook endpoint management. Users can save actions that silently do nothing.
acceptance_criteria: Either implement named webhook configuration, secure persistence, bounded async POST delivery, diagnostics, and failure visibility, or remove/hide the action until supported. No silent no-op remains.

title: Add generic trigger overlay routing action
description: Replace implicit/special-case overlay delivery with a backward-compatible Overlay action. A match must be able to add multiple overlay destinations; each destination receives template-expanded named fields plus presentation hints such as variant, size, color, and icon. DisplayText and Impact remain readable for migration/import compatibility.
design: Action::Overlay carries destination, named data fields, and optional presentation hints. The engine expands all string fields and emits one attributed TriggerOverlayPayload per action. Rendering remains owned by the destination overlay.
acceptance_criteria: Rust/TypeScript schemas round-trip; one trigger match can deliver to two overlays plus TTS; destinations filter a shared trigger-overlay event; Alerts and Impact consume the new contract; legacy DisplayText/Impact packs still work; engine/editor tests cover fan-out and template expansion.
close_reason: Implemented generic attributed Overlay actions, multi-destination fan-out, registry-driven Alerts/Impact configuration, compatibility migration, and tests.

title: Move player-facing log rules out of React handlers
description: Inventory and migrate gameplay-specific raw-message regex/equality logic in Dashboard, Fights, Timers, stance, wishlist, proc detection, hail lookup, and overlays. Shared parsing/aggregation stays in Rust; configurable player-facing reactions and overlay presentation flow through triggers.
design: Rust owns typed facts and stateful reducers. Triggers consume raw lines or typed facts and emit actions. React renders state/action payloads and does not parse game grammar.
acceptance_criteria: Document the parser/aggregator versus configurable-rule boundary; migrate stance/proc/skill/wishlist/hail presentation rules in prioritized slices; no duplicate built-in alert plus trigger behavior; add replay tests for migrated rules.

title: Preserve active timers across trigger engine rebuilds
description: Profile, loadout, or override changes hot-swap the TriggerEngine and currently drop its active timer state. Editing trigger settings mid-session should not erase unrelated running buff/debuff timers.
acceptance_criteria: Engine rebuild preserves compatible active timers and snapshots; removed/disabled timer definitions have explicit behavior; test switching loadouts and channel overrides during active timers.

title: Audit and simplify trigger-driven architecture
description: Review the trigger model, engine, editor, execution pipeline, and overlay consumers. Identify hard-coded log parsing outside the trigger system, ensure matches can route reusable payloads to multiple configurable overlays and TTS, improve trigger management, implement high-confidence fixes, and track remaining architectural work.
design: Treat triggers as the event-routing backbone. Separate match facts from presentation. Overlay destinations should consume typed trigger payloads and apply overlay-specific presentation configuration without hard-coded gameplay parsing.
acceptance_criteria: Document current trigger flow and gaps; identify hard-coded parsing that bypasses triggers; implement scoped high-confidence improvements; add focused tests; all Rust workspace tests, frontend tests/typecheck/build pass; changes committed and pushed.
notes: Completed multi-agent audit across engine/Tauri execution, trigger editor/profile management, and overlay/frontend consumers. Confirmed core strengths: guided pattern builder, multiple ordered actions, live capture/action preview, curated packs, per-loadout controls, and efficient RegexSet matching. Blocking gaps tracked as eqlogs-83d, 8ub, bk9, l7u, 988, a7m, uzq, 5ha, 2pz, 1e2, and 29j with dependency order. No code edited because the working tree already contains a large uncommitted feature set touching the same files; implementation should proceed in isolated claimed issues without absorbing unrelated changes.
close_reason: Audit complete; findings decomposed into prioritized, dependency-linked implementation issues.

title: Update trigger pack checker for event sources
description: tools/spelldata/check_packs.py is stale relative to the current trigger model: it expects 52 packs, rejects ObserveWatch, requires patterns for typed event triggers, and expects only three impact triggers. The app parser loads all 53 packs and frontend/Rust tests pass, but the checker reports nine false failures.
acceptance_criteria: check_packs.py recognizes current pack count, ObserveWatch and typed event triggers, validates the current impact pack shape, and exits successfully on the checked-in trigger library.

title: Show configured icons beside timer bars
description: Render the trigger-configured spell/ability/buff/debuff icon in a stable square column beside dashboard and overlay timer progress bars, similar to EverQuest buff rows, with a restrained aligned fallback.
acceptance_criteria: Timer rows show configured spell-gem or glyph icons without duplicate rendering or layout shift; missing icons use an aligned fallback; warning/pending/expiry states remain coherent; focused tests and production build pass.
notes: Timer rows now render fixed spell-gem/glyph/fallback icon columns in dashboard and overlays; live engine already propagates configured trigger icons; generated buff/debuff packs carry spell references; tests/build pass; deployed to Windows 3.0.1.
close_reason: Implemented, tested, visually verified, and deployed in Windows build 3.0.1.

title: Add generated animated loot chest presentation
description: Replace the current code-drawn watched-loot chest with generated transparent fantasy chest artwork and a trigger-configurable, performant Impact animation with reduced-motion support.
acceptance_criteria: Generated chest asset is bundled and visually verified; Impact loot-chest presentation uses transform/opacity-based arrival, opening flash, glow, particles, item reveal, and reduced-motion fallback; trigger retains presentation control; tests/build pass.
notes: Generated open/closed transparent fantasy chest WebP assets; implemented trigger-configurable intensity, aura/rays/flash/particles/reveal and reduced-motion fallback; visually verified; frontend and production Windows builds pass; deployed to Windows 3.0.1.
close_reason: Implemented, tested, visually verified, and deployed in Windows build 3.0.1.

title: Add watched kill goals for quests
description: Extend the watched-goal system beyond loot so users can watch required NPC kills, see remaining kill counts, receive trigger-driven completion feedback, and manage kill watches alongside watched drops. Determine how quest kill objectives are represented in the bundled catalog and preserve character-scoped storage.
acceptance_criteria: Users can add/remove kill watches, configure required counts and auto-remove behavior, parsed personal/group-relevant slain events decrement exact mob goals without hardcoded quest parsing, watched kills are visible in management UI, tests and Windows checks pass, and the feature is deployed.
notes: Implemented character-scoped kill goals, generic typed Slain -> watch match -> watched-kill trigger flow, modular monster-rip Impact style, Mobs/Quests/Session management, migration/tests, patch notes, and Windows 3.0.1 deployment. Verified 254 frontend tests, full Rust workspace, 10 native Windows watch tests, production build, deployed trigger resources, and tauri.localhost WebViews.
close_reason: Implemented, verified, and deployed in 3.0.1

title: Restore Rust formatting and Clippy gates
description: Recent trigger-sharing tests and documentation fail current cargo fmt and clippy -D warnings, which would make CI fail when the current feature work is pushed. Apply mechanical formatting and resolve the doc/clone lints without behavioral changes.
close_reason: Applied Rust formatting and resolved current Clippy warnings; fmt and clippy gates pass.

title: Polish fight status, stabilize Session navigation, and audit martial abilities
description: After eqlogs-fdz: (1) show player and enemy/mob status distinctly in Fight details, (2) keep Session header and tabs in consistent positions across Last fight/Current session/Past session views, (3) verify and fix Kick, Flying Kick, and other learned martial skills in the Abilities database/UI.
close_reason: Implemented player/enemy fight panels, fixed-position Session navigation and summary, and maintained learned martial skills in Abilities with database validation.

title: Add pet mez parking macro to macro library
description: Add the EverQuest hold-and-park pet control social to the built-in Macros database. It should anchor the pet, engage Hold, clear hate with Back Off, explain toggle behavior and Legends regeneration limits, and be searchable under pet macros.
acceptance_criteria: Macros tab contains a Pet Mez / Hold-and-Park entry with the verified command order, pet classes, source, and safety tips; data/build tests pass.
close_reason: Added and verified the Legends pet tank parking macro using /pet guard, Hold, Back Off, and Stop; added paired resume macro and regression tests.

title: Feature: pack lint before share + version-aware pack re-import with diff
description: Share dialog lint: lookaround regexes (Rust engine rejects), absolute sound paths, hardcoded char names, hot patterns without cooldown, StartTimer without lane. Re-import: optional version/author/notes in SharePayload (serde skip_serializing_if, wire-compatible), added/changed/removed preview, update-in-place preserving per-id overrides.
close_reason: Closed

title: Feature: coin + skill-up parser events and session analytics (plat/hr, faction ledger, skills, trends)
description: Rust: Money event (loot/sell coin lines verified in fixtures/sample_session.txt 796/943/1276/1322/1660) + SkillUp event ('You have become better at X! (63)', currently swallowed parser.rs:945). Frontend: plat/hour in pace, session wallet, faction ledger (Event::Faction parsed but discarded), skill-up panel, progression trends sparklines over CoachTab session history. All per everything-configurable principle.
close_reason: Closed

title: Feature: welcome-back briefing card
description: On start after catch-up: last played N days ago, zone/level/XP, kill rates, camp timers expired while away, wishlist drops since. Read-only stitching of CoachTab session history, lib/timers, lib/wishlist, fight store. Deep-link per line.
close_reason: Closed

title: Feature: zone-scope editing UI for triggers
description: Backend fully supports Trigger.zones + per-loadout zone_scopes + LCS1 transport with tests; zero UI sets it. Add zone chips in TriggerEditor (autocomplete from refdb zones), per-category zone-scope override in loadout view, zone badge in trigger tree.
close_reason: Closed

title: Nav re-cut: Spells+Abilities toggle, Patch Notes under Settings, Settings re-org, single classes/level editor
description: 16→13 tabs: merge Abilities into Spells via segmented toggle; move Patch Notes to Settings→Updates + update-banner link; re-section Settings General by mental model (Log & character / Audio & speech / ...); make Triggers-tab bar the single classes/level editor, Settings Loadouts links to it.
close_reason: Closed

title: Extract DB-tab search scaffold (useDebouncedRefSearch + Pager + SearchSelect.tsx); QuestsTab conformance
description: Drops/Mobs/Recipes/Spells duplicate debounce+paging+expand+pager pipeline (~250 lines). Move SearchSelect/SpecRow out of DropsTab. Fix dead sortable-looking headers on Mobs/Recipes/Spells. Then rebuild QuestsTab on Database conventions: card-head, RefFilters (EraSelect/ClassFilterButton/SearchSelect zone), Empty.tsx, error-banner, pager, drop autoFocus.
close_reason: Closed

title: Shared UI primitives: useToast, Modal, duration formatters, deep-link lib, createLocalStore, class roster
description: 5 toast impls, 5 modal scaffolds (2 without Escape), 8 duration formatters with real drift (respawn overlay drops seconds), stringly deep-link CustomEvents, localStorage quartet x5 libs + raw copies in CoachTab/QuestsTab, 16-class roster x3. Extract shared primitives and adopt everywhere. Also Empty.tsx adoption in Timers/Coach/Diagnostics; error/loading normalization.
close_reason: Closed

title: Ship quest catalog through rolling data updates
description: Extend the existing data-latest pack and updater so refreshed quests.json catalog revisions can ship independently of a full desktop release. Preserve CC BY-SA attribution and validate catalog schema/Sky audit before activation.
acceptance_criteria: Data pack contains versioned quest catalog and attribution; updater validates and atomically activates it; failed updates retain bundled/last-good catalog; UI reports installed catalog revision; tests cover activation and rollback.

title: Validate trigger patterns with production Rust regex
description: The editor tests and validates patterns using JavaScript RegExp while production uses Rust regex, so unsupported constructs can save successfully and fail only after backend compilation.
acceptance_criteria: Save/test calls a backend compile/test command using the production expansion and Rust regex semantics; errors identify the unsupported construct; JS preview is clearly secondary; tests cover lookaround/backreference rejection and GINA tokens.

title: Replace category-name trigger semantics with explicit metadata
description: The engine suppresses friendly casts and routes timers based on category/id substrings such as Enemy Casts, Buffs, Debuff, and /cc/. Renaming or reorganizing a trigger can silently change runtime behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Trigger schema has explicit caster scope and timer destination/lane metadata; editor exposes them where relevant; legacy inference is migration-only and emits diagnostics; tests prove category renames do not alter behavior.

title: Analytics follow-ups: pace coin metric, unknown-character store migration, coin-split MoneyKind
description: (1) Add coin as an explicit pace-sample metric (PaceEvent kind 'money') so Rates start/stop samples capture plat alongside XP/motes — Wallet panel currently owns live plat/hr; (2) faction/skill stores key by config.characterName, events before config resolves land under 'unknown' — migrate-on-config-load pass; (3) verify group coin-split line shape when a grouped log is available, add fourth MoneyKind; (4) welcome-back wishlist line limited to catch-up replay window (SESSION_CAP=200) — could widen via fight-history store; DST boundary skew documented; (5) release note: trends level-up markers only on rows archived after this change; toast timings unified 2600/6000ms.

title: Share follow-ups: export-dialog metadata inputs, .lct update-in-place path, removed-diff rows
description: From the versioned re-import round: (1) ShareDialog export UI has no version/author/notes inputs (wire+CLI+import preview already support them; needs optional args on share_export/share_export_file); (2) .lct file imports cannot take update-in-place because share_read_file strips already-installed ids before preview; (3) import dialog could surface 'removed' diff rows once imports record pack provenance; (4) TriggersTab per-row Reset does not clear exact-id zone_scopes entries (only share imports create them); (5) surface zone scopes in share import preview.

title: Promote 'has been awakened by' line to typed event for exact mez-break attribution
description: Exact attribution exists in logs: 'Baron Telyx V`Zher has been awakened by Nyasha.' (verified twice in fixtures/local/eqlog_full.txt) but is swallowed as sys_flavor (parser.rs ~286). Promoting it to a typed event would replace/confirm the frontend heuristic in lib/mezBreaks.ts — needs dedupe against the heuristic claim to avoid double counting. Also: curated mez timers ('Mez (Mesmerize)') never get target-bound (binding keys on timer name == spell name); consider alias table.

title: Feature: career database + one-time log-history backfill
description: Extend eqlog-store with sessions/xp_events/level_ups/loot tables; 'Import log history' replays existing logs (catchup.rs proves whole-log replay). Unlocks drop-rate ledgers, wishlist ETAs, long trends. LARGE; mind timestamp-domain trap (naive-local-as-UTC). Deferred from review round.
close_reason: Closed

title: Feature: mez-break attribution + CC-breaks scoreboard stat
description: Join enemy-lane mez timer on target T with next damage event on T (scoreboard fold) to attribute breaks. Spoken alert opt-in per everything-configurable; mezBreaks counter on Party Scoreboard.
close_reason: Closed
